bonuses.leadin: An Erwin Panofsky essay argues that this painting's title object was inspired by Norse mythology, in contrast to Theophilus Stringfellow Jr., who argued that it is a "microcosm of man." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this landscape by Thomas Cole that depicts a vast sea, a Greek temple, and architectural ruins within and on the rim of the title object.
Thomas Cole is a member of the school of painting named for this river in New York. Its other painters included Albert Bierstadt and Frederic Church.

In an article about The Titan's Goblet, Ellwood Parry argued that the painting was inspired by this artist's Ulysses Deriding Polyphemus; his other classical-inspired paintings include Ovid Banished from Rome.

parts_sanitized: Name this landscape by Thomas Cole that depicts a vast sea, a Greek temple, and architectural ruins within and on the rim of the title object.
Thomas Cole is a member of the school of painting named for this river in New York. Its other painters included Albert Bierstadt and Frederic Church.

In an article about The Titan's Goblet, Ellwood Parry argued that the painting was inspired by this artist's Ulysses Deriding Polyphemus; his other classical-inspired paintings include Ovid Banished from Rome.

bonuses.leadin: The title structure of this painting stands on a hill dwarfing the tiny human village below. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this painting which depicts what appears to be a gigantic fountain brimming with water.
The Titan’s Goblet was painted by this American landscapist better known for depicting the progression of civilization in his The Course of Empire.

According to the art historian Ellwood Parry, Cole’s painting of The Titan’s Goblet may have been influenced by his viewing of a J.M.W. Turner painting depicting these two mythological characters, the first of whom is mocking the second from aboard a ship.

parts_sanitized: Identify this painting which depicts what appears to be a gigantic fountain brimming with water.
The Titan's Goblet was painted by this American landscapist better known for depicting the progression of civilization in his The Course of Empire.

According to the art historian Ellwood Parry, Cole's painting of The Titan's Goblet may have been influenced by his viewing of a J.M.W. Turner painting depicting these two mythological characters, the first of whom is mocking the second from aboard a ship.

bonuses.leadin: The first three paintings in this series depict an hourglass attached to a boat, and the second entry shows a heavenly castle. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this four-painting series in which an angel guides a man through Childhood, Youth, Manhood, and Death.
This member of the Hudson River School painted The Voyage of Life and The Course of Empire series. He showed a winding river in The Oxbow.
In this Thomas Cole painting, several ships sail in an ocean contained in the oversized title object. Vegetation grows on the title object’s brim, which contains a Greek temple.
